events.label: reasoning
events.text: The connection requires pretensioned A325 bolts. Photo shows bolts with head markings not matching submitted bolt grade. No turn-of-nut match marks or DTI washer indications visible. Crew described joint as "snug tight only." This is non-compliant. Decision: fail. Risk: high or critical. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 70. Need rework: remove and replace bolts with correct grade, pretension per RCSC, inspect.

events.label: response

  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",

  "findings": [
    "Bolt head markings do not match submitted A325 bolt grade; installed fasteners may be wrong grade or unverified.",
    "No visible pretension evidence: no turn-of-nut match marks and no DTI washer indications.",
    "Crew installed joint as snug-tight only where pretensioned A325 bolts are required.",
    "Connection accepted by crew based on visual stability rather than specified pretensioning method."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop acceptance of the connection pending verification.",
    "Identify and remove non-matching bolts; replace with verified A325 bolts matching approved submittal.",
    "Pretension bolts using an RCSC-approved method: turn-of-nut, calibrated wrench, twist-off bolts, or DTI washers.",
    "Apply match marks or install/verify DTI washers to provide pretension evidence.",
    "Document fastener lot, installation method, and inspector verification before closing out the joint."
  ],
  "rationale": "The detail requires pretensioned A325 bolts, but the installed fasteners are unverified by head marking, no pretension evidence is present, and the crew only snug-tightened the joint. This does not satisfy RCSC pretensioning requirements and cannot be accepted.",
  "references": [
    "RCSC Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts",
    "AISC Steel Construction Manual / Specification for Structural Steel Buildings",
    "Approved bolt submittal and shop drawings"
  ]
}
